channel.title: autoregressive on Camille's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in autoregressive on Camille's Blog

item.title: The limits and future of generative models
item.description: A generative model is a model which is designed to generate new data samples from learned data .colored { background-color: rgba(255, 235, 59, 0.6); padding: 0 1px; font-weight: bold; border-radius: 4px; box-shadow: 0 0 8px rgba(255, 235, 59, 0.6); display: inline; line-height: 1.5; } . So a first step in generative modeling is to model the data distribution .colored { background-color: rgba(255, 235, 59, 0.6); padding: 0 1px; font-weight: bold; border-radius: 4px; box-shadow: 0 0 8px rgba(255, 235, 59, 0.
